title: Helix Health - Private installer upgrade blocker triage (stuck at migrations)
preview: Helix Health reported a blocked Redwood Private upgrade where the installer progresses but hangs/fails at the database migrations step. Redwood and Helix walked through what “stuck at migrations” typically means (migrations job pending/crashlooping, database connectivity, schema lock, or resource constraints), align...
text: Helix Health reported a blocked Redwood Private upgrade where the installer progresses but hangs/fails at the database migrations step. Redwood and Helix walked through what “stuck at migrations” typically means (migrations job pending/crashlooping, database connectivity, schema lock, or resource constraints), aligned on a safe diagnostics bundle to collect, and agreed on a conservative rollback plan if forward-fix cannot be validated quickly. The conversation stayed focused on minimizing risk for a compliance-heavy environment and keeping Support/Engineering escalation paths clean (SUP-1845).

title: Containment warroom handoff and stability roadmap - Crestline Logistics
preview: Short bridge to hand back to ops: we reviewed the immediate containment steps taken by Crestline, walked through Redwood's preliminary RCA (likely KV cache thrash + hot-path batching misalignment), agreed on short-term mitigations (rate‑limit and fallback routing), and committed to a joint load test, runbook handoff...
text: Short bridge to hand back to ops: we reviewed the immediate containment steps taken by Crestline, walked through Redwood's preliminary RCA (likely KV cache thrash + hot-path batching misalignment), agreed on short-term mitigations (rate‑limit and fallback routing), and committed to a joint load test, runbook handoff, and a canary rollout plan. Security/scoping questions (audit logs, KMS) to be answered via follow-up doc.

text: Leadership reviewed the 2026 strategic 'beacon' to align squads around a small number of directional experiments, discussed headcount pacing and tight fiscal guardrails, walked through recent reliability incidents and mitigations, and opened the floor to squad-level challenges and lightning demos. Action items around experiment charters, hiring approvals, and a post-mortem schedule were agreed.

title: Controlled Failover Game Day Briefing (prod) - us-east to eu-west

Covered run-of-show, go/no-go gates, signal dashboards to watch, comms plan (internal + support + status page), and explicit rollback triggers. text: Pre-brief for the production controlled regional failover (us-east -> eu-west). Covered run-of-show, go/no-go gates, signal dashboards to watch, comms plan (internal + support + status page), and explicit rollback triggers. Confirmed limited blast radius (selected routes/tenants), dry-run output expectations, and who has authority to approve execution/rollback.

title: Regional failover automation upgrade - kickoff
preview: Kickoff for the Reliability Program workstream to upgrade regional failover automation across Hosted API and Dedicated. Team aligned on problem statement (manual/partial automation, noisy triggers, slow detection, hard to validate), in-scope components (signal pipeline, region health aggregation, controller + determ...
text: Kickoff for the Reliability Program workstream to upgrade regional failover automation across Hosted API and Dedicated. Team aligned on problem statement (manual/partial automation, noisy triggers, slow detection, hard to validate), in-scope components (signal pipeline, region health aggregation, controller + deterministic policy evaluation, guardrails/rollback, observability, runbooks, game days) and out-of-scope items (multi-cloud migration, serving runtime rewrite, SLA language). Open questions focused on trigger hierarchy (authoritative signals vs corroboration), anti-flap behavior, approval model (auto vs ack-required), and how to safely run controlled production failovers with limited blast radius.

Quick security posture discovery focused on hosting options (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private), KMS / BYOK, SAML/SSO integration, RBAC and audit log retention. Customer is a regulated financial services company assessing whether Redwood can fit into their PCI/GLBA-adjacent environment with minimal scope creep.

Topics:
- SOC2/ISO artifacts and scope
- SSO/SAML integration with Okta
- RBAC model and least privilege
- Audit logs: retention, format, access controls
- Encryption: in-transit, at-rest, BYOK/HSM options
- Data residency and logs PII handling
- Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private tradeoffs for scope and ops

Action items (recorded here inl...

title: Urgent: Fallback policy & long-tail error mitigation sync
preview: Customer reported a series of long-tail 5xx/timeout errors affecting checkout traffic during peak. Today alignment focused on containment (immediate fallback/rollback), investigation scope (logs, KV cache checks, prefix caching), and medium-term reliability mitigations (canary, circuit breaker, observability tweaks)...
text: Customer reported a series of long-tail 5xx/timeout errors affecting checkout traffic during peak. Today alignment focused on containment (immediate fallback/rollback), investigation scope (logs, KV cache checks, prefix caching), and medium-term reliability mitigations (canary, circuit breaker, observability tweaks). SE and AE walked through a proposed rollback playbook, discussed tradeoffs between hosted failover vs Dedicated fallbacks, and agreed on short list of actions and timelines.

title: Incident taxonomy working session (v1) - tricky classifications + owner mapping
preview: Working session to finalize incident taxonomy v1 edges and the owner mapping for disputed categories. Team aligned on keeping top-level types small (target ~12) with subtypes where needed. Resolved key disputes: capacity shortage remains Fleet primary; routing/fallback misbehavior remains Smart Routing primary; quot...
text: Working session to finalize incident taxonomy v1 edges and the owner mapping for disputed categories. Team aligned on keeping top-level types small (target ~12) with subtypes where needed. Resolved key disputes: capacity shortage remains Fleet primary; routing/fallback misbehavior remains Smart Routing primary; quota/rate limit enforcement is Control Plane primary (Support consulted for customer-caused throttling); streaming disconnects default to Edge/Gateway unless clear client SDK regression. Agreed to add a simple rule for multi-cause incidents: pick the mitigation owner as primary type, track other systems as contributing factors in postmortem. Observability reiterated cardinality guardrails (allowlist + unknown bucket). Action items captured for updating the decision tree, owner mapping config, and incident-bot suggestions.

title: Helios Defense — Private pilot check-in (signature verification issues)
preview: Helios reports offline install preflight is failing signature verification on the latest pilot bundle after transfer into the air-gapped network. Redwood triages live: likely bundle was modified during unzip/repack (hash mismatch) and there is also an installer version mismatch (older installer doesn’t support the d...
text: Helios reports offline install preflight is failing signature verification on the latest pilot bundle after transfer into the air-gapped network. Redwood triages live: likely bundle was modified during unzip/repack (hash mismatch) and there is also an installer version mismatch (older installer doesn’t support the detached signature layout + updated trust store). Agreed on a clean re-transfer method, validating checksums before/after copy, and upgrading the installer to the required build. Redwood to resend the bundle + provide exact commands and expected output, and Helios to share the failing logs and the install host’s system time configuration.

text: Acme Bank security team reviewed Redwood Inference’s customer-managed KMS integration for Private deployments, focusing on envelope encryption, rotation workflow (including dual-decrypt window and rollback), and what audit evidence Redwood can provide. Acme emphasized quarterly rotation, segregation of duties, and clear behavior during KMS degradation/outage. Redwood committed to sharing the Private KMS operator guide, rotation procedure/evidence checklist, and a sample audit-log export schema including rotation events, plus scheduling a follow-up deep dive on failure-mode behavior and operational runbooks.

Demo covered request logs, trace view, error categorization, request playback, and linking traces to model variants and resource metrics. Primary customer concerns: reproducibility of failures, audit trails for SOC2, and whether playback reproduces KV-cache dependent issues. Agreed next steps: share security docs, run a 24-hour capture for production traffic, schedule a latency benchmarking PoC and follow up with cost/throughput options.

title: Northbridge Capital Markets - Private deployment security review (model change audit + release notes)
preview: Northbridge reviewed Redwood Private change-management controls focused on: (1) audit logging for model metadata updates and version promotions, (2) retention and SIEM export options, and (3) how customer-visible release notes are generated without leaking internal details. Redwood explained the v1 model metadata sc...
text: Northbridge reviewed Redwood Private change-management controls focused on: (1) audit logging for model metadata updates and version promotions, (2) retention and SIEM export options, and (3) how customer-visible release notes are generated without leaking internal details. Redwood explained the v1 model metadata schema and event-driven changelog generator, including public vs internal fields, RBAC, and idempotent eventing. Northbridge asked for: exact event fields (actor, approval, diff), retention guarantees in Private, offline/air-gapped export, and assurance that pinned versions cannot be changed silently. Next steps: Redwood to share an example audit event + sample release note artifact, confirm Private retention configuration and SIEM forwarding patterns, and provide a short write-up mapping release notes to upgrade bundles/rollback documentation.

title: Polaris Bioinformatics — payload transfer, offline license activation & runtime tuning
preview: Deep dive on air-gapped artifact transfer, offline license activation and runtime tuning for on-prem deployment. Focus areas: secure payload handoff (sideload vs sealed tape), checksum/signing and verification, NVIDIA driver bundle compatibility, KV cache warmup strategies, batching/latency tradeoffs, quantization p...
text: Deep dive on air-gapped artifact transfer, offline license activation and runtime tuning for on-prem deployment. Focus areas: secure payload handoff (sideload vs sealed tape), checksum/signing and verification, NVIDIA driver bundle compatibility, KV cache warmup strategies, batching/latency tradeoffs, quantization profiles and suggested baseline for their genomics inference workloads. Agreed next steps: provide signed artifact checklist, driver bundle with SHA256, run a 48-hour warmup test on a small cluster, and schedule pilot kickoff (week of Oct 6).

title: Evacuation routing for private deployments
preview: Technical deep dive focused on multi-region routing behavior for private (VPC/on-prem) deployments. Primary goals: design an interruption-tolerant routing policy that minimizes cold-start costs, preserves KV-cache locality where possible, defines quantization fallback lanes for capacity-constrained regions, and docu...
text: Technical deep dive focused on multi-region routing behavior for private (VPC/on-prem) deployments. Primary goals: design an interruption-tolerant routing policy that minimizes cold-start costs, preserves KV-cache locality where possible, defines quantization fallback lanes for capacity-constrained regions, and documents an evacuation (failover) runbook for BeaconMed's private clusters. Discussed tradeoffs between latency and cost, warmup sequencing, and audit requirements for cross-region fallbacks.

Team identified optimization opportunities (batching config and prefix/KV cache) and agreed to a short benchmark. Security questions about VPC/private control plane, SSO/SAML and audit logs were raised; AE to share compliance docs. SE will reproduce a slow tail request and surface policy-level fallback options.
